title: A Stacky Perspective on P-adic Non-abelian Hodge Theory
speaker: Arthur-Cesar Le Bras
abstract: P-adic non abelian Hodge theory, also known as the p-adic Simpson correspondence, aims at describing p-adic local systems on a smooth rigid analytic variety in terms of Higgs bundles. I will explain in this talk why the « Hodge-Tate stacks » recently introduced by Bhatt-Lurie and Drinfeld in their work on prismatic cohomology can be useful to study this kind of questions. Joint work with Johannes Anschütz and Ben Heuer.

proof_stages.label: Classical template and p-adic problem formulation
proof_stages.summary: The talk recalls the complex non-abelian Hodge correspondence between representations and Higgs bundles, then asks for a p-adic analogue on a smooth proper rigid space while noting that the usual universal-cover construction of flat bundles is already problematic rigid-analytically.

proof_stages.label: v-vector bundles as generalized representations
proof_stages.summary: To replace the missing rigid universal cover, the speaker passes to diamonds and the v-site, defines v-vector bundles as finite locally free O-modules for that fine topology, and identifies these with Faltings-style generalized representations to be compared with Higgs bundles.

proof_stages.label: Why only restricted correspondences are expected
proof_stages.summary: By comparing v-vector bundles with analytic vector bundles and recalling prior small/local p-adic Simpson correspondences, the talk establishes that a global equivalence for all objects is too optimistic and that liftability and smallness/topological conditions must enter.

proof_stages.label: Canonical functor from the Hodge-Tate stack
proof_stages.summary: Using the Drinfeld–Bhatt–Lurie Hodge-Tate stack of a p-adic formal scheme and its functoriality on perfectoid test objects, the speaker constructs the central functor alpha from perfect complexes on the Hodge-Tate stack, up to isogeny, to perfect complexes on the v-site of the generic fiber.

proof_stages.label: Smooth-case full faithfulness via local Hodge-Tate-stack models
proof_stages.summary: For smooth formal schemes, full faithfulness of alpha is reduced locally to an explicit description of the Hodge-Tate stack as a classifying stack built from the divided-power tangent group, followed by an explicit algebraic computation; the arithmetic variant is briefly revisited in Q&A.

proof_stages.label: Identification of the source with topologically nilpotent Higgs data
proof_stages.summary: The local classifying-stack description translates coherent sheaves on the Hodge-Tate stack into modules with topologically nilpotent symmetric-algebra action, equivalently formal Higgs bundles with commuting, topologically nilpotent Higgs fields, yielding a local fully faithful Higgs-to-v functor.

proof_stages.label: Globalization through liftability and obstruction gerbes
proof_stages.summary: The Hodge-Tate stack is treated as a gerbe whose pushout measures the obstruction to lifting modulo the square of the relevant period ideal, so a liftability assumption gives a global fully faithful functor only after imposing a stronger topological condition on Higgs fields, with Q&A clarifying the generic-fiber formulation and independence from the chosen formal model.

examples.name: Complex Simpson correspondence and the degree-one abelian case
examples.note: The complex equivalence between fundamental-group representations and semistable Higgs bundles, together with ordinary Hodge decomposition in degree one, is used as the motivating template for the p-adic problem rather than as an input to the proof.
examples.engagement_reason: The speaker recalls Simpson’s complex correspondence and the degree-one Hodge decomposition as contextual facts without computing or deriving them on a concrete case.

passes.reason: The cited H^1/G_a case is the abelianized base case motivating the complex Simpson correspondence, not a refutation.

examples.name: The point Spa(k) for a p-adic field
examples.note: For the simplest rigid space Spa(k), v-vector bundles become finite-dimensional continuous Gal(k)-representations over the completed algebraic closure, forcing the conclusion that one cannot expect an equivalence with all Higgs bundles.
examples.engagement_reason: For X = Spa(k) the speaker compares analytic bundles with v-bundles, pulls back to the completed algebraic closure, and identifies the resulting category with finite-dimensional continuous Gal_k representations over C.

passes.reason: Spa(k) is a zero-dimensional/minimal analytic space used to show the naïve all-objects p-adic Simpson equivalence already fails.

The point Spa(k) is a minimal rigid-analytic base and is used to show that v-vector bundles can already differ from analytic vector bundles.

examples.name: Scholze’s higher direct image computation for O_v
examples.note: The calculation R^i nu_* O_v = Omega^i(-i) for a smooth rigid space illustrates that the v-structure sheaf remembers differential forms and is therefore genuinely different from the analytic topology.
examples.engagement_reason: Scholze’s higher direct image result for O_v is cited and its outcome in degree i is stated, but no computation is carried out in the talk.

passes.reason: Scholze’s formula for higher direct images of O_v is a standard structural computation illustrating extra v-site information.

Scholze’s nonzero higher direct images of O_v are invoked as a witness that the v-site does not behave like the ordinary analytic site.

examples.name: One-dimensional divided-power affine line
examples.note: When the module of differentials is free of rank one, the divided-power tangent group is described as the affine line with divided powers, making the local classifying-stack model concrete without being needed as a separate proof case.
examples.engagement_reason: The speaker specializes the divided-power envelope construction to the one-dimensional free case, identifying it with the affine line G_a equipped with divided powers.

examples.name: Ogus–Vologodsky characteristic-p comparison

examples.engagement_reason: Ogus–Vologodsky is brought up only in the question discussion as a related characteristic-p comparison, with no concrete verification or construction performed.
